name: Ace of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: New financial beginnings, material opportunity, groundedness, and the tangible seed of prosperity held in open palms.
reversedMeaning: Missed opportunity, poor planning, financial instability, or an attachment to security that prevents risk and growth.

name: Two of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Balance, adaptability, time management, and the graceful juggling of multiple priorities in constant flux.
reversedMeaning: Overwhelm, disorganization, imbalance, or the first signs that too many plates are about to fall.

name: Three of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Teamwork, skill, collaboration, and the quiet pride of craftsmanship when talent meets purposeful effort.
reversedMeaning: Lack of collaboration, poor workmanship, or misaligned efforts that undermine what could have been built together.

name: Four of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Security, conservation, control, and the careful stewardship that protects what has been earned — sometimes to excess.
reversedMeaning: Releasing control, generosity, or finally loosening the grip on what was clutched too tightly.

name: Five of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Hardship, financial struggle, isolation, and the profound test of faith when the warm glow of the window seems just out of reach.
reversedMeaning: Recovery from hardship, renewed hope, or recognizing that help and warmth have always been available nearby.

name: Six of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Generosity, giving and receiving, balance in material exchange, and the flow of abundance shared with open hands.
reversedMeaning: Charity with strings attached, inequality, debts that bind, or generosity withheld from miserly fear.

name: Seven of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Long-term vision, patience, investment, and the quiet faith of one who tends what was planted and trusts the season.
reversedMeaning: Impatience, poor investment, lack of long-term thinking, or effort expended in the wrong direction.

name: Eight of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Diligence, mastery in progress, craftsmanship, and the satisfaction of deep work practiced with devotion.
reversedMeaning: Perfectionism, misdirected effort, lack of focus, or repetition without growth.

name: Nine of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Luxury, self-sufficiency, refinement, and the elegant harvest of one who cultivated abundance through disciplined effort.
reversedMeaning: Financial setbacks, over-dependence, or the trappings of success acquired at the cost of genuine fulfillment.

name: Ten of Pentacles
uprightMeaning: Legacy, permanence, family wealth, and the deep satisfaction of building something that endures beyond a single lifetime.
reversedMeaning: Financial failure, family conflict, instability, or legacy built on shaky or hollow foundations.
